title: Ageratina dolichobasis
taxonomy: Ageratina dolichobasis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Mexico and is part of the genus Ageratina, which is a group of shrubs and subshrubs commonly known as mountain silver or false ageratum. The plant is further classified as a member of the tribe Eupatorieae. The classification of Ageratina dolichobasis is also based on its characteristics, including its growth habit, leaf shape, and flower arrangement. This classification helps scientists to understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and its place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Ageratina dolichobasis, also known as Mexican plume, is a perennial plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) characterized by its showy, pinkish-purple flowers and tall, erect stem. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ageratina dolichobasis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
